title: Bourj Hammoud · sea landfill

classification.label: quarry_expansion
classification.reasoning: The before image shows a dark area with minimal visible features, while the after image reveals exposed light-colored rocky/sandy terrain with what appears to be excavated material and infrastructure. This pattern of dark vegetated/undisturbed land transitioning to bright exposed rock/earth with rectangular cut features is characteristic of quarry expansion, which is common in Lebanon's mountainous regions.

flags: quarry-like change outside any permitted zone

factors.label: quarry-like change outside any permitted zone

second_opinion.reason: The before image shows a dark vegetated or shadowed surface while the after image reveals a bright, highly reflective bare surface with structured edges consistent with quarry expansion or excavation activity, not explainable by seasonal, shadow, or tidal changes.

narration: Monitoring Bourj Hammoud sea landfill — 2017-04-01..2017-10-31 vs 2024-05-01..2024-09-30.
Pulled before/after composites and aligned them (same-season, cloud-masked).
Detected 21.92 ha of change at 33.9023 N, 35.5547 E (NDVI drop -0.396, BSI rise 0.198).

Classified as quarry_expansion (confidence 0.72; claude). Second opinion: confirmed — The before image shows a dark vegetated or shadowed surface while the after image reveals a bright, highly reflective bare surface with structured edges consistent with quarry expansion or excavation activity, not explainable by seasonal, shadow, or tidal changes..
Legality check: within the coastal public-domain setback; quarry-like change outside any permitted zone.
Drafted an alert for human review — not sent.
